reference: Methodology: Zanzonico et al. J Nucl Med 40(6):906-915, 1999 (bremsstrahlung exposure from pure beta emitters). NOTE: that publication tabulates specific bremsstrahlung constants for soft tissue (Z=7.9) and bone (Z=21) only — the container-specific values below (PMMA/Pb/W/none) are estimates following its formalism and are NOT tabulated in the publication.
notes: Estimated dose rate constants for bremsstrahlung from Y-90 beta particles in different container geometries. Values are μSv·h⁻¹·GBq⁻¹·m². Pending verification against a primary source; do not use for regulatory submissions without independent verification.

containers.name: PMMA (acrylic) container
containers.description: Syringe or vial in PMMA; standard geometry

containers.name: Lead (Pb) container
containers.description: Shielded in lead; higher atomic number increases bremsstrahlung
containers.warning: ⚠️  Lead increases bremsstrahlung significantly (3–4×) compared to PMMA. Use PMMA or plastic for Y-90 shielding, or accept increased external dose from bremsstrahlung.

containers.name: Tungsten (W) container
containers.description: High-Z alternative shielding (better than Pb for beta)
